message:
My neighbor Sarah showed up to our block's power outage last week with a camping lantern, a cooler full of ice she'd prepped months ago, and somehow even a little battery-powered radio. I was sitting in my dark kitchen eating warm ice cream straight from the melting container, and she was like "Hey, want some light?" 

I used to tease her about being *that person* — you know, the gloriously over-prepared one with the go-bag in her hall closet and the water stored in her garage. But watching her calmly help her elderly parents and check on us? I got it. Being ready isn't about doom-scrolling or catastrophizing. It's just... being the person who can actually *help* when something shifts.

I finally dug out that old preparedness checklist we made fun of her for. Turns out even a little planning feels less like paranoia and more like peace of mind.

What's one small prep thing you've been meaning to tackle? (No judgment — we've all got our Sarah moments waiting to happen.)
